source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 3 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Independent repairers can escape a candidate set whose correct answer is absent or underrepresented.
strategies.name: Five Solvers plus Three Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: Expanding the shared independent bank from five to nine may improve exact-answer coverage and stabilize candidate support, while retaining the only review mechanism with a positive observed correction balance.
strategies.name: Nine Solvers plus Three Falsifying Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: Pooling the three falsifying repairs with the five independent base votes may preserve useful base evidence while allowing convergent repairs, or repairs matching a correct minority candidate, to win.
strategies.name: Five Solvers with Fused Repair Voting

strategies.hypothesis: When the base has no strict majority, three frequency-blind comparative reviewers may recover correct minority candidates such as those exposed by the sequence oracle gap without disturbing stronger base consensus.
strategies.name: Guarded Three-Way Candidate Adjudication
